title: crataegus monogyna var. granatensis
intro: Crataegus monogyna var. granatensis is a variant of the common hawthorn tree, a deciduous shrub or small tree native to Europe, North Africa, and Western Asia, characterized by its thorny branches and small, white flowers. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, where its berries, leaves, and flowers are used to treat cardiovascular and gastrointestinal issues due to their antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and vasodilatory effects. The health benefits of Crataegus monogyna var. granatensis include reduced blood pressure, improved heart health, and relief from anxiety and insomnia, making it a popular herbal remedy for cardiovascular and mental health concerns. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, tinctures, and infusions made from the berries, leaves, and flowers, which can be consumed fresh or dried for medicinal purposes.
